machinery_genes.quote: Each of the three P. aeruginosa T6SSs encodes a VipA and a VipB homologue, namely HsiB1 and HsiC1 (H1-T6SS), HsiB2 and HsiC2 (H2-T6SS), and HsiB3 and HsiC3 (H3-T6SS)
machinery_genes.note: Lossi et al 2013 (J Biol Chem) name HsiB3 as the H3-T6SS VipA/TssB sheath subunit. The paper structurally characterizes the H1 pair HsiB1C1 and explicitly states the H3 orthologues HsiB3/HsiC3 form the equivalent sheath; the three Hsi pairs are non-redundant. PAO1 strain.

machinery_genes.quote: Each of the three P. aeruginosa T6SSs encodes a VipA and a VipB homologue, namely HsiB1 and HsiC1 (H1-T6SS), HsiB2 and HsiC2 (H2-T6SS), and HsiB3 and HsiC3 (H3-T6SS)
machinery_genes.note: Lossi et al 2013 name HsiC3 as the H3-T6SS VipB/TssC sheath subunit; same sentence also notes 'HsiC1, HsiC2, and HsiC3 have pairwise sequence identities between 38 and 46%' and predicted gp18-like (tail-sheath) C-termini. PAO1 strain.

machinery_genes.quote: The secretion of Hcp3 was similarly reduced but not abolished in these mutants
machinery_genes.note: Lin et al 2017 (Nat Commun) use Hcp3 as the secreted Hcp tube of the PAO1 H3-T6SS; its secretion is the functional readout for H3-T6SS activity (reduced in core-component deletion mutants). Hcp is machinery per the type brief (secreted tube), not an effector. PAO1 strain.

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike / tube tip (core component, secreted)
machinery_genes.quote: On the chromosome of P. aeruginosa strain PAO1, tseF is situated at the distal end of the H3-T6SS locus and is next to vgrG3, which codes for a core component of the H3-T6SS
machinery_genes.note: Lin et al 2017 explicitly call vgrG3 'a core component of the H3-T6SS' in PAO1, located at the distal end of the H3-T6SS locus adjacent to the effector tseF (PA2374). VgrG is machinery (spike) per the type brief, not the delivered effector. PAO1 strain.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contracted sheath (core component)
machinery_genes.quote: clpV3 encodes the ClpV AAA+ ATPase, a core component of the secretion machinery
machinery_genes.note: Sana et al 2013 (PLoS ONE) name clpV3 as the H3-T6SS ClpV/TssH AAA+ ATPase, a core component; the PAO1 H3-T6SS mutant used in that work is a clpV3 deletion. Independently confirmed as the H3-T6SS ClpV by Lin 2017 (clpV3 complementation amplified from PAO1 genomic DNA) and by the ClpV3 Frontiers study (10.3389/fmicb.2020.01096). PAO1 strain.

notes: System: the H3-T6SS (HSI-III) is one of three Type VI secretion systems in P. aeruginosa PAO1 (H1/H2/H3); it delivers toxin effectors via a contractile phage-tail-like apparatus and is the system to which this instance's effectors (PA2374/TseF, PA4922, PA5089) belong. PA2374 (TseF) is the H3-T6SS effector characterized by Lin et al 2017 and sits at the distal end of the H3-T6SS locus next to vgrG3. Locus organization (Sana et al 2013): the PAO1 H3-T6SS is two divergent operons ('left' and 'right') plus the vgrG3-tseF operon; the left/right intergenic region lies between PA2364 and PA2365, and the locus begins around PA2359. STATUS = PARTIAL on purpose. I could find verbatim, strain-attributable naming quotes for five core apparatus genes: the two sheath subunits HsiB3/HsiC3 (Lossi 2013), the secreted tube Hcp3 and the spike VgrG3 (Lin 2017), and the ATPase ClpV3 (Sana 2013). The remaining conserved T6SS components are present in the H3 cluster (Sana 2013 and Boyer 2009 establish that PAO1 T6SS loci carry the full ~13-gene Tss core), BUT I could not find a published quote naming the individual H3-locus baseplate genes (TssE/F/G/K = HsiF3/G3 etc.) or the membrane-complex genes (TssJ/L/M, IcmF3/DotU3) with an exact gene name in extractable text; their H3-specific names appear only inside figure panels (e.g. the Sana 2013 Fig 2 locus map labels hsiA3-J3 / hsiB3-H3), which the full-text extractor strips. Per the quote-or-omit rule I did NOT list them rather than pad. PAAR is named only generically as a T6SS tip component in the PA14 review (10.1186/...PMC10442045) and could not be pinned to a specific H3 gene, so it is excluded. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the delivered effectors TseF/PA2374, TepB, PA4922, PA5089 and any immunity proteins; regulators (RpoN/Sfa2/Sfa3, QS LasR/RhlR, RsmA, AmrZ, MvfR/PQS, PhoB/PitA). All listed quotes are verbatim from PMC full text or the cited DOI; all four PMIDs/DOIs verified via get_article_metadata. No MacSyFinder/HMM/Pfam scan and no GenBank mining were used. Lowest-confidence call: none of the five are low confidence; the gap is in unlisted (omitted) baseplate/membrane genes, which is why status is PARTIAL not COMPLETE.
